case_name: The MEDICAL CENTER OF CENTRAL GEORGIA, INC. v. NORKESIA TURNER

summary.brief: The MEDICAL CENTER OF CENTRAL GEORGIA, INC. v. NORKESIA TURNER. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Allen Turner died due to complications from a surgery to remove a polyp from his intestines.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Turner alleged that Thompson and Nolan’s negligence in treating Allen—which fell beneath their professional standard of care—caused and contributed to his injuries and death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On March 31, 2017, Drs. Thompson and Nolan performed the operation, during which they unexpectedly discovered a cancerous mass approximately 25 percent larger than a golf ball.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At some point during surgery, one of the doctors clamped the SMA on both sides and cut it in half.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Turner attached an affidavit from a medical expert, Dr. Marvin Evans, to her complaint in support of the allegations.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: The jury rendered a verdict in Turner’s favor, awarding her approximately $9,200,000 in damages—$7,200,000 of which were noneconomic damages for wrongful death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the jury’s verdict in favor of Turner.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The noneconomic damages caps in OCGA § 51–13–1 violate the right to a jury trial as guaranteed under the Georgia Constitution.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Scheduled for surgery to remove a polyp in the distal duodenum
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Referred for surgery to excise a polyp in the very distal duodenum. Underwent endoscopy, colonoscopy, and small bowel follow-through series prior to surgery.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Polyp in distal duodenum requiring surgical excision

requestables.name: CT Scan
requestables.description: CT scan of the abdomen to assess the location of the SMA and any masses

reveal.clinical_significance: Could have revealed the unusual location of the SMA and the presence of a cancerous mass
reveal.decision_impact: Might have prevented the severance of the SMA during surgery

requestables.name: MRI
requestables.description: MRI of the abdomen to assess the location of the SMA and any masses

reveal.clinical_significance: Could have revealed the unusual location of the SMA and the presence of a cancerous mass
reveal.decision_impact: Might have prevented the severance of the SMA during surgery

timeline_phases.name: Preoperative Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Patient assessed and prepared for surgery to remove a polyp

key_events.event: Endoscopy, colonoscopy, and small bowel follow-through series performed

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Surgery to remove polyp and discovered cancerous mass

key_events.event: Discovery of cancerous mass and abnormal SMA location
Multiple additional surgeries due to SMA severance

decision_points.clinical_context: Pre-surgical planning for excision of a polyp in the distal duodenum
decision_points.prompt: Should imaging be performed to assess the anatomy of the abdomen before surgery?

options.description: Order a CT scan of the abdomen
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care to identify anatomical variations and potential complications

options.description: Proceed with surgery without additional imaging

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with surgery without additional imaging

expected_action_court.description: CT scan should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated it would have revealed critical anatomical details

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order imaging led to unanticipated complications during surgery
explanation.what_should_have_happened: CT scan would have identified the abnormal SMA location
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from multi-system failure after multiple surgeries

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to order pre-surgery imaging
Causation established - imaging would have prevented SMA severance

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Imaging would have shown the abnormal SMA location

factual_timeline.event: Surgery performed by Drs. Thompson and Nolan

tests_performed.result: Performed, but no CT or MRI

treatments.treatment: Surgical removal of polyp and cancerous mass

allegations.allegation: Failure to order pre-surgery CT scan

court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to order imaging led to fatal complications
